title: tetrapilus neriifolius
intro: Tetrapilus neriifolius, also known as the Chinese club Moss or Hairy Club Moss, is a type of lycophyte plant that belongs to the family Selaginellaceae. It is traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as protecting against oxidative stress. Tetrapilus neriifolius is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts for its medicinal use, making it a popular ingredient in traditional Chinese medicine.
